steps.title: Identify the tense signal
steps.content: The first sentence places the action firmly in the past: "On March 27, 2004… Girgrah retired." The blank continues the same sentence about that completed 2004 event, so the verb must be simple past tense to match "retired."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'finish': Present-tense form; also does not agree in number with the singular subject "The Yemeni American athlete." Both errors rule it out. ✗
  • Choice B — 'has been finishing': Present perfect progressive, which implies an action still ongoing. Girgrah's career ended in 2004 — it is not still finishing. ✗
  • Choice C — 'finished': Simple past tense; matches "retired" and correctly expresses an action completed at a specific past time. ✓
  • Choice D — 'will be finishing': Future progressive, which contradicts the 2004 date already given in the passage. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
